What CityPulse Does

City data is everywhere.
Insight is the missing layer.

Every signal linked by parcel number. Intelligence delivered the moment city systems update.

Live Data

Permits & Hearings

Building permits and planning hearings pulled directly from city systems — not three months later in a PDF report.

AI Layer

AI Briefings & Signals

Claude reads raw data and writes what it means — severity-rated concerns, emerging trends, and a 90-day forward outlook in plain language.

Spatial

Parcel-Level Intelligence

Every signal linked to a building via its Assessor Parcel Number — the universal join key that makes all city data talk to each other.

Business

Business Activity Tracking

2,710 active businesses tracked live. Month-over-month trends, category breakdowns, and spatial correlation with transit and 311 data.

Mobility

Transit Scoring

Standards-based accessibility score using the Vale 2015 / EPA distance-decay model across an 800m pedestrian shed, weighted by stop type.

Reporting

Board Packet Automation

One-click AI-generated briefing documents for board meetings. Replaces hours of manual assembly with a live, accurate report on demand.

The Platform

Three interfaces.
One intelligence layer.

The same live data — delivered to district managers, property owners, and city planners in the format that works for them.

District Intelligence

A live command center for Business Improvement Districts

Replaces the quarterly manual report with a real-time dashboard. Every morning: live business counts, transit overlays, AI briefings, and spatial correlation insights — ready to share with your board.

  • 2,710 active businesses tracked live from SF Business Registrations
  • Month-over-month opening and closure trend charts
  • 17 transit-rich underperforming blocks identified automatically
  • One-click board packet generation via AI
Open live platform
citypulse.app/cbd/downtown
Business Intelligence

Live ground-floor business tracking — replacing manual quarterly counts

Know what's opening, what's closing, and where momentum is building — before your quarterly report is even drafted. Category filters, spatial overlays, and AI-generated category insights on demand.

  • 637 new businesses in 90 days across Downtown SF
  • Category breakdown: Professional, Food & Bev, Retail, Arts, Health
  • 7 growth momentum corridors identified automatically
  • AI category insight narrative generated on demand
See business activity
citypulse.app/cbd/downtown → Business Pulse
3D Intelligence

CityPulse data synced to a photogrammetric 3D city model

Every building in the Nextspace 3D environment carries a full CityPulse intelligence profile. Click any building to see live permits, business activity, transit score, and AI signals — in three dimensions.

  • CC3D photogrammetric building models for all of San Francisco
  • CityPulse intelligence packages sync via Pull API
  • Ask Nextspace AI natural-language spatial queries
  • Full city binding in progress — FiDi live today
UAT — full city model coming soon
fastcast.op.nextspace-uat.net
What's Live Right Now

This is a soft launch.
Here's exactly what's working.

We're in active development. These features are running in production today on real San Francisco data.

Downtown SF Business Portal
Live

2,710 businesses tracked live via DataSF. Month-over-month trend charts, category breakdown, AI insights, and spatial correlation analysis.

web-five-omega-87.vercel.app/cbd/downtown →
Property Intelligence Dashboard
Live

Any SF building by Assessor Parcel Number. No login. Live permits, 311, businesses, transit score, and AI redevelopment signal.

Try 1 Market St → /property/3713007 →
Transit Scoring
Live

Standards-based walk-access scoring using Vale 2015 / EPA Smart Location Database distance-decay model. 104 stops scored. 1 Market St: 100/100.

Nextspace 3D Integration
UAT

CityPulse intelligence packages parsing correctly in Nextspace UAT. FiDi 72-footprint 3D map live locally. Full city binding pending CC3D APN export.

AI Briefings
Live

Claude generates district briefings, business category insights, and redevelopment signals on demand. Responses cached per session. Costs scale with usage.

Oakland / Bay Area Expansion
Next

County-first architecture means Alameda County (Oakland) reuses ~70% of SF code. Santa Clara County and Dell Public Sector pilot cities to follow.

We're onboarding civic leaders, district managers, and development professionals first. San Francisco now. Bay Area next.
